questions.question: For a normally distributed variable with a known mean μ = 10 and known standard deviation σ = 2, what is the type of distribution used when you want to estimate the mean using the sample mean, and what is the formula for the standard error of the mean in this case?
questions.hint: The formula for the standard error of the mean (SEM) is given by SEM = σ / sqrt(n)

questions.question: A random sample of 36 students is taken from a population with a mean height of 175 cm and a known standard deviation of 10 cm. Calculate the 95% confidence interval for the mean height of the population using the student-t distribution with 35 degrees of freedom. (Assume a significance level of 0.05)
questions.hint: Use the formula for the confidence interval: CI = x̄ ± t * (s / sqrt(n)) * z * sqrt(1 - (1 - α)^2 / (n - 1))

questions.question: A binomial distribution with n = 100 and p = 0.5 is given. Calculate the probability that exactly 12 heads occur when 100 coins are flipped. (Round your answer to 4 decimal places)
questions.hint: Use the binomial probability mass function: P(X = k) = C(n, k) * p^k * (1 - p)^(n - k)
